rationale: The skill is a CLI wrapper around Chrome/Chromium via CDP for browser automation, so it will spawn the agent-browser binary (or via npx) and launch a browser subprocess. Browser automation implies arbitrary network access to any user-specified site, plus screenshot/state writes to disk and reads from session/auth vault config. Installation via npm and the 'skills get' subcommands suggest reading/writing local config and cache directories. Path and host predicates are broad because the skill's verbs (navigate, screenshot, scrape) target arbitrary user-chosen URLs and local artifact paths.
